title: Review staged Beads snapshot after scaffold refactor commit
description: Inspect the currently staged generated change in .beads/issues.jsonl after commit ae25d1b to determine whether the change should have been auto-staged and committed with that work, and identify the mechanism gap if it was missed.
design: Compare HEAD, index, and working tree around .beads/issues.jsonl plus relevant git hook and Beads export/bootstrap integration paths. No product code changes unless a concrete fix is approved.
acceptance_criteria: - Explain what the staged change contains and why it appeared after the last commit\n- Determine whether the previous commit should have included it automatically\n- Identify the exact mechanism or workflow gap responsible
notes: Evidence will come from git status, cached diff, last commit stat, and repository hook/config inspection.

title: Document frontend navigation graph
description: Document the frontend application's page navigation graph in docs/navigation-graph.md, including connected and disconnected pages discovered from the current frontend routing and navigation surfaces.
design: Inspect the frontend app's route/page definitions and in-app navigation entry points, then write a Markdown graph and page inventory to docs/navigation-graph.md. Include pages with no inbound or outbound links as disconnected nodes.
acceptance_criteria:
- docs/navigation-graph.md exists and describes the current frontend page graph
- Connected routes are represented with their navigation edges
- Disconnected pages are listed explicitly rather than omitted
notes: Requested directly by the user. Initial repository scan needed to locate the frontend app and its route definitions.

title: Backfill retroactive Beads tasks for existing git history
description: Retroactively create high-quality Beads tasks for each historical git commit in this repository, with exact commit timestamp backdating and a retcon label, without changing git history.
design: Inspect git log in chronological order, analyze each commit diff, create one Beads issue per commit, backdate created/updated/closed timestamps to the commit timestamp, and close each issue immediately. Check existing Beads data first to avoid duplicate retcon tasks.
acceptance_criteria: - Every historical git commit without an existing retcon issue gets one corresponding Beads task\n- Each created task is labeled retcon and backdated to the exact commit timestamp for created_at, updated_at, and closed_at\n- A final report lists the number of tasks created and any commits skipped as already covered
notes: Requested via /fabricate-beads-history on 2026-04-28. Need to verify whether earlier runs already created any retroactive tasks before backfilling.

title: Draft conventional commit message for current staged changes
description: Inspect the currently staged git changes and provide a conventional commit message that matches the actual scope and intent.
design: Use git diff --cached to inspect staged files and summarize the change as a conventional commit without modifying code or git state.
acceptance_criteria: - Identify whether staged changes exist\n- Produce a conventional commit header\n- Include a concise body when the staged scope warrants it
notes: Initial probe: git diff --cached returned no output; verify staged state before drafting the message.

title: Fold leaked beads export-state into prior commit and stop restaging it
description: A Beads-generated .beads/export-state.json change was staged at commit submission time instead of being included in the commit that triggered it. Fix the current leaked change and adjust the workflow/config so this file does not keep becoming a post-commit annoyance.
design: Inspect the last commit, beads export-state generation path, git ignore/tracking rules, and commit hooks. Amend or otherwise repair the prior commit to include the leaked file if appropriate, then change the repo config or hook behavior so export-state does not reappear as a staged leftover after commits.
acceptance_criteria:
- The leaked .beads/export-state.json change is no longer left staged separately from the commit it belongs to
- Future commits do not leave .beads/export-state.json staged as a follow-up annoyance
- Any hook/config change preserves the intended beads workflow
notes: Observed immediately after commit bdb58a2 with only A .beads/export-state.json left staged.

title: Summarize staged scaffold/bootstrap changes for commit
description: Review the current staged scaffold/bootstrap change set and produce a conventional commit message that reflects the actual scope. No code changes; scope is limited to inspecting staged diffs and naming the change accurately.
design: Inspect git status and cached diff for scaffold, bootstrap, skill, template, and beads metadata updates. Derive a single commit message that matches the staged contract without expanding scope.
acceptance_criteria:
- Commit subject uses conventional commit format
- Subject reflects the staged scaffold/bootstrap work
- Body captures the main why/scope in a few lines if needed
notes: Staged diff shows scaffold/bootstrap split, new bootstrap/scaffold scripts, skill/template updates, and beads metadata changes.

title: Replace interactive bootstrap prompts with inferred bootstrap skill
description: Change the shell bootstrap entrypoint so it no longer interrogates the user for project metadata and commands. It should infer what it can and generate the bootstrap artifacts non-interactively. Add a new bootstrap skill that performs the deeper project interrogation by reading the codebase and using the grill-me approach to fully define any remaining project context.
design: Update scripts/bootstrap.sh and universal templates. Introduce a bootstrap skill template and generated copies where appropriate. Adjust docs and workflow references so the shell command handles generation while the new bootstrap skill handles agent-side discovery and project-context refinement.
acceptance_criteria:
- bootstrap.sh runs without the old question-and-answer prompts for project metadata
- a new bootstrap skill is generated and documented
- the bootstrap skill explains how to inspect an existing codebase and derive project context using grill-me style interrogation of the code rather than the human
notes: Created from user request to separate file generation from project interrogation and move the latter into a new skill.

title: Explain repository structure and architecture
description: Inspect the repository, identify its current structure, purpose, and architecture, and provide a concise explanation grounded in the existing files and conventions.
design: Review README.md, AGENTS.md, top-level configuration, and src/ if present. Summarize module responsibilities, workflows, and notable gaps without changing code.
acceptance_criteria:
- Claimed issue exists for this exploration work
- Project summary covers purpose, structure, and current state
- Notes any missing or not-yet-configured areas discovered during inspection
notes: Created because bd ready --json returned no available tasks for this explanatory walkthrough.

title: Unify scaffold apply and refresh behavior around scaffold state
description: Refactor the scaffold flow so both initial application and later refreshes are driven by the same scaffold-state model instead of diverging command paths. Remove the obsolete sync-bootstrap workflow, align harness skills and templates around the new refresh behavior, and simplify documentation and tests to match the state-driven contract.
design: Update scaffold state artifacts, harness skill docs, AGENTS/CLAUDE templates, scaffold.sh, and smoke tests so apply and refresh share one implementation path with consistent semantics.
acceptance_criteria:
- Scaffold apply and refresh run through the same state-driven logic
- Obsolete sync-bootstrap artifacts are removed from generated outputs and templates
- Documentation and smoke tests reflect the unified scaffold contract
notes: Retconned from commit ae25d1b597abfd84942394e91352330746dcf282 (2026-04-28T18:24:02Z): 'refactor(scaffold): unify apply and refresh around scaffold state'.

title: Verify Beads readiness during scaffold bootstrap instead of trusting generated files
description: Fix the scaffold workflow so it distinguishes between generating Beads-related files and actually having an operational local Beads store. The bootstrap path should attempt a non-destructive Beads bootstrap when the CLI is available, clarify the recovery path in generated documentation, and harden template rendering and smoke coverage around this distinction.
design: Update scaffold.sh, the universal AGENTS/bootstrap/history/retro templates, and the scaffold smoke test. Verify that placeholder rendering is escaped correctly and that runtime Beads artifacts do not cause false failures in smoke coverage.
acceptance_criteria:
- Scaffold bootstrap checks for actual Beads readiness and documents bootstrap recovery explicitly
- Template rendering is hardened for replacement values
- Smoke tests ignore Beads runtime artifacts and catch placeholder leakage
notes: Retconned from commit 35515cf24f87798a1dbd0eee64d8872482dae4c0 (2026-04-28T18:09:27Z): 'fix(scaffold): verify Beads readiness during scaffold bootstrap'.

title: Add a durable ubiquitous language glossary for scaffold terminology
description: Create a shared glossary that defines the project’s canonical terms for scaffold behavior, durable artifacts, workflow stages, and Beads integration. The goal is to reduce instruction drift across plans, templates, and agent interactions by making the preferred vocabulary explicit in one place.
design: Add .claude/context/ubiquitous-language.md and populate it with repository-grounded terminology, definitions, and source references.
acceptance_criteria:
- The repository contains a glossary of canonical terms under .claude/context/ubiquitous-language.md
- Entries cover the key scaffold, workflow, and Beads concepts used elsewhere in the project
notes: Retconned from commit 735739bac3f38bebd878c28782472c9996b17acc (2026-04-28T15:24:14Z): 'docs(context): add ubiquitous language glossary'.

title: Document the bootstrap manifest contract and harden project bootstrap guidance
description: Consolidate the project’s own design context around the bootstrap manifest and the long-term direction of agent-bootstrap. Add a durable charter and manifest specification, deepen the grill-me workflow, and strengthen bootstrap and smoke-test behavior so the repository’s internal contract is explicit rather than implicit.
design: Add durable planning documents under .claude/, refine the grill-me skill, update bootstrap.sh around the documented manifest behavior, and broaden smoke-test coverage to reflect the richer contract.
acceptance_criteria:
- The repository has durable documentation for the bootstrap manifest contract and project charter
- Bootstrap workflow guidance and smoke-test coverage are updated to align with that documented contract
notes: Retconned from commit e3b60d3b9da2b350cdc7ce6eba862b03e658cf51 (2026-04-26T23:59:21Z): 'conventional commit message for the changes'.

title: Bootstrap Beads configuration for the agent-bootstrap repository itself
description: Adopt Beads in this repository, not just in the downstream scaffold templates. Track the repository-local Beads files, add ignore rules for local runtime state, install repo-level hooks, and configure the project so future work can be managed through Beads directly.
design: Add .beads documentation, metadata, config, and hook scripts; update top-level ignores and agent instructions; and wire any repository-local settings needed to make Beads operational here.
acceptance_criteria:
- This repository contains the Beads bootstrap files and hook scripts needed for local operation
- Repo documentation and ignores reflect the new Beads integration
notes: Retconned from commit 58e12a3377dff46bce4792e9281b9f741fc63eb1 (2026-04-26T23:16:39Z): 'chore(beads): bootstrap repository Beads configuration'.

title: Generate Antigravity skill outputs and align cross-harness documentation
description: Bring the generated Antigravity scaffold up to parity with the existing Claude and Codex skill sets, and use that expansion to clean up repository guidance that had drifted across harnesses. This includes generating the new Antigravity skills, simplifying the bootstrap manifest representation, and refining shared documentation and architecture guidance so all harness outputs stay aligned.
design: Add Antigravity skill files, refresh generated harness-specific outputs, and update AGENTS/template documentation where the cross-harness contract has evolved.
acceptance_criteria:
- Antigravity receives generated skill files for the core workflow set
- Shared documentation and generated harness outputs are updated to stay consistent with the new cross-harness shape
notes: Retconned from commit c437597a501fc00936ccdd7854fd59c8cee52f25 (2026-04-25T02:15:22Z): 'feat: add antigravity skills and update documentation with refined test configuration and architectural guidance'.

title: Clarify timestamp fidelity and task-quality expectations in the history backfill template
description: Tighten the fabricate-beads-history template so retroactive task generation does not drift into shallow or inaccurately timestamped issue records. The guidance should explicitly push for stronger task descriptions and precise backdating behavior.
design: Edit the universal fabricate-beads-history template only. Refine the wording around backdating requirements and the level of effort expected when reconstructing task intent from commits.
acceptance_criteria:
- The history backfill template more clearly requires exact timestamp fidelity
- The template explicitly raises the expected quality bar for reconstructed task descriptions
notes: Retconned from commit 85c89c88518083e1546ac590dd63c0fa865eac63 (2026-04-24T23:36:26Z): 'docs: refine instructions for backdating and improving the quality of task descriptions in history templates'.

title: Add Antigravity as a first-class scaffold target
description: Extend the script-based bootstrap flow so it can generate artifacts for Antigravity alongside the existing harness targets. Keep the selection model and documentation aligned, and prove the new target with the existing smoke test coverage.
design: Update AGENTS guidance, bootstrap script branching, README target descriptions, and smoke tests to include Antigravity-specific output generation.
acceptance_criteria:
- Bootstrap supports Antigravity as an explicit target
- Documentation and smoke tests both reflect the new target
notes: Retconned from commit 0a6f084a6365dab5ba226f12bfca6a32540870c3 (2026-04-24T20:57:49Z): 'feat(bootstrap): add Antigravity bootstrap target'.

title: Replace the bootstrap skill entrypoint with a script-based bootstrap command
description: Move project bootstrap generation out of the Claude-only skill implementation and into a repository-owned shell script so the scaffold can be applied consistently from the command line. Preserve the generated outputs while making bootstrap execution reproducible and tool-independent.
design: Remove the previous bootstrap skill implementation, add scripts/bootstrap.sh as the primary entrypoint, and refresh README guidance so users invoke the script rather than relying on a specific harness-side skill implementation.
acceptance_criteria:
- Bootstrap generation is driven by scripts/bootstrap.sh
- Prior bootstrap behavior is represented in the script and supporting docs rather than the removed Claude-only skill file
notes: Retconned from commit 30670e2635e5c1852e45fc22c2cb861a62911d38 (2026-04-24T20:41:57Z): 'feat(bootstrap): add script-based project bootstrap'.

title: Expand the scaffold around design-first workflow artifacts and smoke coverage
description: Shift the generated agent workflow upstream toward shared design alignment before coding. Add durable artifacts for planning, glossary work, architecture improvement, and TDD guidance across the generated Claude and Codex outputs, and add a smoke test that proves the template-managed files and workflow markers remain internally consistent.
design: Update universal templates and the generated local scaffold to include feature-start, grill-me, improve-architecture, tdd, ubiquitous-language, and workflow guidance. Add a bootstrap manifest and smoke-test script to validate the generated artifact set.
acceptance_criteria:
- Generated scaffold includes design-first skills, durable artifact guidance, and workflow stages before implementation
- A smoke test exists to verify generated bootstrap artifacts and expected workflow markers
notes: Retconned from commit 4a933c80b4c1310ab8f91f0d53a66cf4e6e49252 (2026-04-23T20:13:46Z): 'feat(bootstrap): add design-first workflow templates and smoke test'.

title: Add a dedicated history-backfill skill for retroactive Beads adoption
description: Provide a reusable skill that can reconstruct Beads history for repositories that already have a meaningful git timeline before adopting task tracking. The skill should direct the agent to inspect commits chronologically, infer the original work intent, and create high-quality retroactive tasks instead of shallow commit-message copies.
design: Register a fabricate-beads-history skill template and update bootstrap instructions so scaffolded repos can invoke it when adopting Beads after the fact.
acceptance_criteria:
- A fabricate-beads-history skill template exists in the universal scaffold
- Bootstrap instructions mention or generate the new skill for downstream repos
notes: Retconned from commit 2a711eca9b4c1ce93adb34751a651430d2a86f30 (2026-04-21T18:04:24Z): 'feat: add fabricate-beads-history skill template and registration'.

title: Integrate Beads task tracking and hook wiring into generated scaffolds
description: Teach the bootstrap system how to scaffold repositories that use Beads as the mandatory task tracker. This includes the clone contract, config files, gitignore rules for local Beads state, and hook scripts that keep exported issue snapshots synchronized with git commits.
design: Extend both bootstrap instructions and universal templates with .beads assets and git hook helpers. Keep the generated workflow explicit about claiming a Beads task before starting work.
acceptance_criteria:
- Generated scaffolds include the core .beads files and hook scripts needed for repository-local Beads usage
- Bootstrap instructions explain the Beads workflow and clone bootstrap contract
notes: Retconned from commit a11566ba91819315c645671dd4bdde37ebec13fc (2026-04-21T17:48:16Z): 'feat: integrate beads task tracking support into bootstrap templates and workflows'.

title: Add multi-provider scaffold support with shared AGENTS guidance
description: Refactor the generated instruction set so Claude-specific behavior and tool-agnostic repository guidance are no longer conflated. Introduce shared AGENTS.md output, keep CLAUDE.md focused on Claude-specific behavior, and let scaffold generation target Claude Code, Codex, or both from a single bootstrap flow.
design: Add an AGENTS template, update the bootstrap skill to collect a tool target, and gate output generation by provider selection. Preserve shared project overview and working agreements across providers while avoiding duplicated instructions.
acceptance_criteria:
- Bootstrap generation can target Claude Code, Codex, or both
- Shared repository guidance is emitted via AGENTS.md for Codex/both targets
- CLAUDE.md is reduced to Claude-specific content when both outputs are present
notes: Retconned from commit 11cbf06cf59b185150687c381b62f90388415be4 (2026-04-21T17:03:25Z): 'feat(bootstrap): add multi-provider support for Codex CLI and AGENTS.md'.

title: Scaffold the first universal workflow templates and bootstrap skill
description: Create the initial reusable template set that turns the bootstrap concept into generated artifacts. This work establishes the bootstrap skill plus shared templates for CLAUDE guidance, implementation roles, anti-patterns, feature-start, retro, sync-bootstrap, and the feature workflow so downstream repositories can be scaffolded consistently.
design: Add the bootstrap skill definition and a first pass of universal template files under bootstrap-templates/templates/universal/. Favor a complete vertical slice of the workflow over stack-specific customization.
acceptance_criteria:
- The repository contains bootstrap templates for core instructions, skills, agents, and workflow guidance
- A bootstrap skill exists to drive generation of those artifacts
notes: Retconned from commit 7808e2b44b6b42b89dac5cbb4ee226ce868dfd6 (2026-04-21T16:45:32Z): 'feat: add universal bootstrap orchestration system'.

title: Define the universal bootstrap orchestration concept in the project README
description: Expand the repository README from a placeholder into a concrete product overview for a tech-agnostic bootstrap system. Document the bootstrap skill, universal templates, staged feature workflow, retrospective loop, and the sync path so new contributors understand the intended operating model before any code generation details are introduced.
design: Keep implementation documentation-first in README.md. Capture the end-to-end orchestration model, the role of templates and skills, and how learnings propagate back into the bootstrapper.
acceptance_criteria:
- README explains the universal bootstrap system and its major workflow stages
- Documentation is detailed enough for a reader to understand the product direction without reading templates first
notes: Retconned from commit bf06738476122e58cca144310e2a1af0235df6a9 (2026-04-21T16:45:10Z): 'feat: add universal bootstrap orchestration system'.

title: Seed the repository with a minimal README placeholder
description: Create the initial README so the repository has a visible project entrypoint before the scaffolded bootstrap system lands. Scope is intentionally minimal: establish the file and make the repo non-empty/documented enough for follow-on work.
design: Add a top-level README.md placeholder only. Keep the initial commit narrow so subsequent commits can expand the project contract deliberately.
acceptance_criteria:
- Repository contains a top-level README.md
- Change is limited to establishing the initial documentation entrypoint
notes: Retconned from commit d58125fa659e07bcaf55981810e8681a2a9099ce (2026-04-21T16:18:25Z): 'Create README.md'.
